title: activity — artisan commands
description: Artisan commands this module ships. Registered by `ActivityServiceProvider::registerCommands()`. Support tooling audience — walk activity for a specific user / entity, count volume for capacity planning, trigger the retention pipeline manually during incidents, run the one-shot install migration.

commands.description: Prints activities matching filters as a table. `--subject` accepts `type:id` (e.g. `booking:bkg_01H...`). `--since` / `--until` accept ISO-8601 or natural expressions ('yesterday', '1 hour ago'). Useful during support tickets when the tenant-facing UI doesn't surface enough context.
Prints the full row + resolved subject + resolved causer + every sibling activity sharing the same batch_uuid. Debug tool for 'what exactly happened during that request'.

Counts activities matching filters. `--group-by` accepts `log_name`, `subject_type`, `causer_id`, `application_id`, `day`, `hour`. Powers capacity-planning + audit-adjacent volume reports without a full row scan.

One-shot Wave-6 install migration. Reads `tenant_id` out of the vendor's `properties` JSONB blob and populates the new dedicated column. `--dry-run` counts affected rows without writing. `--verify` runs a companion check confirming zero rows remain with tenant_id IS NULL AND properties->>'tenant_id' IS NOT NULL.

Dispatches PurgeExpiredActivitiesJob. Optional per-tenant / per-log_name / per-age scoping. Called nightly by the scheduler + on-demand during incidents. `--dry-run` counts rows that would be deleted without actually deleting.

Creates or updates an ActivityRetentionPolicy from the CLI. Requires --reason with at least 20 chars (matches the online API). Used mostly for regulator-hold interventions that need to bypass the UI. Delegates to the API's write path so the same guards fire.

Bulk export activities to a file. Alternative to the HTTP export endpoint for large tenants where the async job time-boxes out. Streams rows to `--output` (or stdout when omitted). `--format` accepts `csv`, `json`, `parquet`.
